repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest API entry Q1_SpectralThreshold is a Markov/FWL sign-lock and row-space refutation, and banked q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov is an event-window singular-value threshold, not Honest-DiD sensitivity inference

published_prior_art.cite: GalNedoma1972 and BemporadMorariDuaPistikopoulos2002 give active/critical-region structure; RambachanRoth2023 and AndrewsRothPakes2023 give fixed-M Honest-DiD/linear CMI inference

repo_prior_art.cite: none; API ExactID/PartialID catalogues are seed-empty and Q1 panel entries do not treat RR/ARP critical values or tied Honest-DiD faces

published_prior_art.cite: FangSantos2019 covers directionally differentiable inference; FirpoGalvaoParker2023 covers value-function inference; KaidoMolinariStoye2022 covers constraint-qualification regularity

repo_prior_art.cite: none; pid_did_anticipation_bounded/v1 is independent-box anticipation partial ID, not Honest-DiD breakdown-value inference

published_prior_art.cite: RambachanRoth2023 notes one-sided inference for M_star; MastenPoirier2020 develops breakdown-frontier inference; FangSantos2019 covers nonsmooth plug-in laws

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1--3
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the kernel is plausibly useful but currently reads as an application-specific composition of multiparametric programming, fixed-M RR/ARP inference, generic nonsmooth/value-function inference, and breakdown-frontier inference rather than a flagship-level new econometric principle.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The tied conditioning event C_Q imposes exact equalities among continuous Gaussian face scores, so the conditional quantile is undefined unless the proposal specifies a regular conditional law on the affine tie subspace or a tube-limit conditioning scheme.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The regular-face delta law and tied inversion need an explicit nonzero/transversal crossing condition on the derivative or one-sided slopes of chi_k at M_star; Assumption quotient_est gives consistency of estimated objects but not identification of a crossing rate.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the tied Gaussian law through a regular conditional Gaussian distribution on the tie hyperplane, or through an explicit vanishing-tube limit, and restate Conjectures 2--3 with that object.
Add a transversality assumption for M_star: monotone first crossing, nonzero regular derivative, and tied one-sided slopes with at least one inward and one outward active direction.

To plausibly reach flagship tier, strengthen the claim from local Honest-DiD bookkeeping to a uniform-in-M coverage theorem or implementable generic active-face certificate for a broad class of conditional moment-inequality sensitivity paths.

recommended_next_step: Revise the tied conditioning and crossing-rate assumptions, then decide whether the kernel can be lifted above field tier with a uniform pathwise inference theorem.
